growth_records.cite: Dexter et al. Bacterial catabolism of acetovanillone, a lignin-derived compound. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2213450119. [PMC9618137]
growth_records.snippet: GD02 grew on liquid minimal medium supplemented with OCF extract up to a concentration of 2 mM total aromatic compounds. The doubling time of the bacterium was ∼9 h
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (~9 h) explicitly reported in text (Fig. 5 growth curve); growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/doubling_time = ln(2)/9 = 0.077 h-1. Oxygen status (aerobic) inferred from organism physiology and OD600-monitored M9+minerals batch culture at 30C; not explicitly labelled 'aerobic' in text (methods do not state shaking/aeration explicitly), hence medium confidence.

growth_records.cite: Dexter et al. Bacterial catabolism of acetovanillone, a lignin-derived compound. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2213450119. [PMC9618137]
growth_records.snippet: which was faster than on 2 mM vanillin (∼15 h) but slower than on 2 mM vanillate (∼3.3 h)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (~15 h) on 2 mM vanillin explicitly reported in comparison sentence in Results. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/15 = 0.046 h-1. Temperature/medium/oxygen assumed same as the OCF-extract experiment described in the same paragraph (M9+minerals, 30C); not independently restated for the vanillin condition.

growth_records.cite: Dexter et al. Bacterial catabolism of acetovanillone, a lignin-derived compound. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2213450119. [PMC9618137]
growth_records.snippet: which was faster than on 2 mM vanillin (∼15 h) but slower than on 2 mM vanillate (∼3.3 h)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (~3.3 h) on 2 mM vanillate explicitly reported. growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu = ln(2)/3.3 = 0.210 h-1. Temperature/medium/oxygen assumed same as the OCF-extract experiment described in the same paragraph (M9+minerals, 30C); not independently restated for the vanillate condition.
